reasoning: The FRED linear model and seasonal baseline indicate a 31% chance of an increase for the March observation over the February observation. Historically, this series showed a decrease between February and March in the prior year (2.536 down to 2.440). Treasury 30-year yields have slightly increased in January, but with expected rate cuts priced in down the line, long-term inflation expectations may remain flat or trend slightly downwards. I have nudged the probability up slightly to 35% to account for recent upward momentum (6 consecutive increases before February), but the base statistical estimate points to a probable decrease.
